Article.ArticleTitle: Effects of protein-glutaminase on structural and functional properties and the interaction mechanism in casein micelle suspensions: convergence of multiscale experiments and molecular dynamics simulations.

Abstract.AbstractText: Understanding protein glutaminase (PGase)-driven structural and functional remodeling of micellar casein suspensions is critical. Deamidation (0-40 min) increased the surface charge (ζ potential: -22.2 to -29.8 mV), reduced the particle size (403 nm to 179 nm; transmission electron microscopy verified fragmentation to 50-250 nm nanoparticles), and decreased the surface hydrophobicity to achieve complete solubility (93.49 %). Secondary structure reorganization increased the α-helix (from 20 % to 23 %) and β-sheet (from 14 % to 25 %) contents, increasing the thermal denaturation temperature (106.6 °C), emulsifying activity (from 52 m2/g to 108 m2/g), and oil-binding capacity (from 15.95 % to 38.99 %), and improved proteolytic digestibility. Liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) peptide mapping revealed isoform-resolved PGase-susceptible glutaminyl residues, consistent with glutamine-to-glutamic acid conversion (Gln to Glu), revealing distinct deamidation site distributions across αs1-, αs2-, β-, and κ-casein. Molecular dynamics simulations and docking studies elucidated isoform-specific interactions of PGase with αs1-, αs2-, β-, and κ-casein, revealing conserved energetic hotspots.
